Review started — inspected planner extension, interview TUI, skill, prompt, fleet plan.ts, PLAN.md, settings.json, package.json, tests

Package integration: .pi/settings.json lists all three extensions (fleet, browser, planner) via relative ../extensions/* paths; package.json pi manifest points at ./extensions, ./skills, ./prompts, ./themes. skills/planner.md and prompts/plan.md are discovered by those globs, so /planner and /plan are exposed automatically without manual wiring. tsc --noEmit passes; node --test on extensions/fleet/plan.test.ts passes all 6 tests.

Questionnaire independence: there is no separate questionnaire extension in the tree. The planner implements its own questionnaire TUI in extensions/planner/interview.ts using only @mariozechner/pi-tui primitives (Editor, Key, matchesKey, truncateToWidth) and @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent ExtensionUIContext. The planner does NOT import from any questionnaire extension, so a standalone questionnaire extension (if later added) can stay enabled/disabled independently without affecting /planner.

Mode semantics: /planner is a command-driven entrypoint, not a modal toggle. Session state is guarded by `plannerSession?.phase === 'active'` and awaitingResponse is explicit ('draft'|'challenge'|null). No global plan-mode is entered, so regular chat keeps working. session_start handler offers resume/abandon, /planner handler offers resume/new when a session is live. This satisfies the 'not a classic plan-mode toggle' requirement.

FINDING (HIGH): Coupling to fleet internals. extensions/planner/index.ts line 32 imports loadValidatedPlan from '../fleet/plan.js'. Task 006 requirement says 'the new planner resources are packaged cleanly' and the overall design says planner 'must not require fleet internals to run'. Because planner directly reaches across extension folders, you cannot ship/enable planner without the fleet extension sibling directory present. Recommended fix: either (a) vendor the small PLAN.md validator into the planner folder, (b) expose fleet's plan utilities via an exported package entrypoint and re-import by package name, or (c) invoke a fleet command via pi's extension API instead of a cross-folder import. The ./fleet/index.ts already re-exports these symbols, so the planner should at least consume from there rather than reaching into plan.js directly.

FINDING (MEDIUM): Numbering bug in buildChallengePrompt (extensions/planner/index.ts lines 311-312). The abbreviated (spike/mvp) branch lists items '1.' then jumps to '4.'. The LLM will likely be confused by the missing 2/3 and still try to answer five-item structure. Renumber to 1./2., or use bullets.

FINDING (LOW): Dead interpolation in buildChallengePrompt line 306: `${abbreviate ? 'spike/mvp' : ''} depth` is inside `if (abbreviate)` so the conditional is tautological. Cosmetic but suggests copy-paste; simplify to the literal string 'spike/mvp'.

FINDING (MEDIUM): Doctrine/behavior mismatch on security auto-elevation. skills/planner.md says 'Auto-elevate floor to internal depth' for security work, and DEPTH_DESCRIPTIONS shows 'auto-elevating to Internal for security work' next to spike/mvp. But the code (extensions/planner/index.ts lines 683-697 and 441-447) only WARNS and allows the user to proceed with spike/mvp. The docs and the visible depth menu lie about behavior. Either make it a true auto-elevation (flip depth='internal' after the confirm=No path and re-ask), or rewrite the description to say 'not recommended — confirm required' so it matches reality.

FINDING (MEDIUM): No tests cover the planner module. classifyTopic(), depthOptions(), extractDepthKey(), buildDraftPrompt(), buildChallengePrompt(), buildDeeperChallengePrompt(), getInterviewQuestions(), runInterview() all have zero coverage. classifyTopic in particular has regex pitfalls ('port' triggers migration, 'fix' triggers bug regardless of context) that could silently misroute a goal into the wrong topic and therefore the wrong interview bank. Recommend at least unit tests for classifyTopic (canonical inputs per topic), getInterviewQuestions (depth conditionals), and the security auto-elevation path.

FINDING (MEDIUM): Overengineering risk at spike/mvp depth from fleet validator. validatePlanDocument (extensions/fleet/plan.ts) enforces: overview >= 40 words AND >= 2 sentences, description >= 80 chars AND >= 2 sentences AND 'concrete surface' AND 'acceptance language' for every task. A 3-task spike whose tasks are honestly terse will be rejected until padded. This pushes back against the skill's own spike stance of 'short, aggressive task list' and 'skip without guilt'. Consider relaxing for spike/mvp: either scale the thresholds by depth or allow an explicit `⚠ skipped (spike)` annotation to satisfy the acceptance-language check.

FINDING (LOW): classifyTopic ordering. 'fix authentication vulnerability' is caught by the bug branch first and never reaches the security branch. Reorder security above bug, or require both-signal scoring, so that security-flavored bugs go through the security interview + security auto-elevation guard.

FINDING (LOW): pendingPlanValidation is cleared at the start of agent_end but there is no guard if loadValidatedPlan throws before the user notify chain returns — it already notifies 'validation failed' so this is fine, but note that pendingPlanValidation is never re-armed for a retry. The user sees the error and must re-invoke finalize through /planner; document this recovery path or wire a small 'Revalidate' command.

FINDING (LOW): plannerSkillCache is keyed only by first-seen cwd (line 353-365). If the same pi session is reused across two different project roots, the second project silently reuses the first project's skills/planner.md content. Cache by cwd, or reload on every session start.

FINDING (LOW): Refinement flow (showReviewMenu 'Refine' branch) calls pi.sendUserMessage with raw refinement text and sets awaitingResponse='draft'. There is no wrapper that reminds the LLM it is still in the planner session or tells it to keep the depth profile — it relies entirely on before_agent_start re-injecting the skill every turn. This works, but the refinement turn would benefit from a `[PLANNER REFINEMENT — Round N]` header mirroring buildDraftPrompt so the LLM does not drift into chatty prose.

FINDING (LOW): The challenge prompt's 'Challenges & Tradeoffs' section is explicitly stripped by the finalization prompt before writing PLAN.md. That's good for fleet compatibility, but the tradeoff analysis is a genuinely valuable artifact. Consider writing it to .pi/tasks/plan-notes.md or a `## Challenges & Tradeoffs` appendix below the fleet-required sections (fleet's validator ignores unknown trailing sections).

FINDING (MEDIUM): Missing user-facing docs. README.md only documents the browser extension. There is no section describing /planner, /plan, the depth profiles, or how PLAN.md hands off to /fleet:split. Task 006 explicitly asks to 'Document how to use the planner, how the planning-depth profiles behave, and how the generated PLAN.md is intended to feed fleet.' Recommend adding a Planner section to README.md (and optionally extensions/planner/README.md) with: command surface (/planner, /plan <goal>), state machine summary, depth table, and the finalize→/fleet:split handoff.

FINDING (LOW): /plan prompt template (prompts/plan.md) uses `/planner $1` but prompt templates typically expand $* for the full argument string. If pi substitutes $1 as only the first whitespace-delimited token, a multi-word goal like `/plan add rate limiting` would collapse to `/planner add`. Verify pi prompt variable substitution semantics; if $* is the correct token, switch to `/planner $*`.

FINDING (LOW): Security-depth skill section is thorough (STRIDE, compliance, least-privilege, audit log, pentest, IR plan). But at internal/production depth the skill does not require a dependency/CVE audit task or a secrets-in-code check. For production depth, add 'Dependency/CVE scan' and 'Secrets management audit' to the required-task list — these are cheap and catch the most common real production incidents.

FINDING (INFO): Positive notes. Clean separation of concerns: interview.ts is pure TUI, index.ts is state machine + prompt building + finalization. State machine comments at the top of index.ts are accurate and helpful. Embedded planner skill fallback means the extension still works if skills/planner.md is deleted. Fleet plan validator (plan.ts) has deterministic topological ordering and six well-targeted unit tests (all passing). /planner correctly gates on ctx.hasUI. The depth menu pre-fills options differently per topic (e.g. spike depth is marked N/A for non-spike topics), which is a nice UX touch. sendUserMessage-based flow cleanly reuses the LLM's own context window instead of reimplementing a planner engine.

Review summary: 1 HIGH (fleet coupling breaks 'independent packaging'), 4 MEDIUM (numbering bug in challenge prompt, doc/behavior mismatch on security auto-elevation, no planner tests, validator vs spike-depth overengineering, missing user docs), 6 LOW (dead interpolation, classifier ordering, cache key, refinement header, tradeoffs artifact discarded, $1 vs $*), 1 INFO (positive notes). End-to-end workflow is functional — PLAN.md round-trips through validator, typecheck passes, existing tests pass. Merging is reasonable after fixing at least the HIGH and the MEDIUM numbering bug; the docs gap and auto-elevation mismatch should be fixed before this ships as a user-facing feature.
